questions.q: From which city did Rama depart to begin his exile?
questions.explanation: Rama left his home city of Ayodhya, the capital of the Kosala kingdom, to begin his forest exile.

questions.q: For how many years was Rama sentenced to live in exile?
questions.explanation: Rama was exiled to the forest for fourteen years as demanded by Kaikeyi through her boon.

questions.q: Who was Rama's wife who chose to share his exile?
questions.explanation: Sita, Rama's devoted wife, insisted on accompanying him into the forest rather than staying behind in comfort.

questions.q: Which brother accompanied Rama and Sita into the forest?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana, deeply loyal to Rama, chose to leave Ayodhya and serve his brother throughout the exile.

questions.q: Why did Rama agree to go into exile instead of refusing?
options: To avoid a war with Bharata
questions.explanation: Rama accepted exile so that his father Dasharatha would not have to break the vow he had made to Kaikeyi.

questions.q: Who was the king and father of Rama whose vow led to the exile?
questions.explanation: King Dasharatha of Ayodhya was Rama's father, bound by the boons he had once promised to Kaikeyi.

questions.q: Which queen demanded Rama's exile?
questions.explanation: Queen Kaikeyi, prompted by her maid Manthara, demanded Rama's exile so her son Bharata could rule.

questions.q: For whose coronation was Rama's exile demanded so the throne would be free?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi demanded the throne for her son Bharata, which required Rama's removal from Ayodhya.

questions.q: Into what kind of place was Rama exiled?
questions.explanation: Rama's exile sent him to live in the forest wilderness, away from the comforts of royal life.

questions.q: What did Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana wear instead of royal garments during the exile?
questions.explanation: To live as ascetics, the three exiles wore garments of tree bark and deerskin in place of royal attire.

questions.q: Whose maidservant first poisoned her mind against Rama, setting the exile in motion?
questions.explanation: Manthara, Kaikeyi's hunchbacked maidservant, convinced the queen to demand Rama's exile and Bharata's crowning.

questions.q: Which charioteer drove Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana away from Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Sumantra, Dasharatha's trusted minister and charioteer, drove the exiles out of the city on their journey.

questions.q: Who was the friendly tribal chief of the Nishadas who helped Rama cross the Ganga?
questions.explanation: Guha, the Nishada chieftain and friend of Rama, ferried the exiles across the Ganga during their journey.

questions.q: At whose hermitage did Rama and his companions stay early in the exile, near the confluence of rivers?
questions.explanation: The sage Bharadwaja received Rama at his hermitage near Prayaga, the confluence of the Ganga and Yamuna.

questions.q: On which hill did Rama and Sita settle and build a dwelling early in the exile?
questions.explanation: Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana settled at Chitrakuta hill, where they built a leaf hut and lived for a time.

questions.q: Lakshmana left his own wife behind in Ayodhya to join the exile. Who was she?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana's wife Urmila, Sita's sister, remained in Ayodhya while he accompanied Rama to the forest.

questions.q: When Rama refused the throne, who came to the forest to plead with him to return and rule?
questions.explanation: Bharata, distressed at his mother's actions, traveled to Chitrakuta to beg Rama to return and take the throne.

questions.q: What did Bharata take back to Ayodhya to place on the throne in Rama's absence?
questions.explanation: Bharata carried Rama's sandals (paduka) back and placed them on the throne, ruling as a regent in Rama's name.

questions.q: From where did Bharata rule the kingdom during Rama's exile, refusing to live in the palace?
questions.explanation: Bharata governed from the village of Nandigrama outside Ayodhya, living austerely until Rama's return.

questions.q: What grievous event happened in Ayodhya shortly after Rama left for exile?
options: Bharata seized the throne by force
questions.explanation: Unable to bear the separation from Rama, King Dasharatha died of grief soon after the exile began.

questions.q: Which of these was a reason Sita gave for insisting on accompanying Rama into exile?
options: A wife's place is at her husband's side
She wanted to meet the forest sages
questions.explanation: Sita argued that a devoted wife's duty and place was beside her husband, in the forest as much as in the palace.

questions.q: After leaving Chitrakuta, in which forest did Rama spend much of the exile, encountering many sages?
questions.explanation: Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana wandered through the vast Dandaka forest, visiting hermitages and protecting sages.

questions.q: Which two boons had Dasharatha once granted Kaikeyi, which she used to force the exile?
options: Wealth and a long life for Bharata
The throne for Bharata and exile for Rama
Half the kingdom and a palace
A war victory and a sacred horse
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi invoked two old boons: one demanding the throne for Bharata and the other demanding fourteen years of exile for Rama.

questions.q: On what past occasion had Dasharatha earlier promised Kaikeyi the two boons she later claimed?
options: After she nursed him back to health in a battle against the asuras
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi had saved Dasharatha's life on a battlefield while he fought the asuras, and he granted her two boons in gratitude.

questions.q: Which queen, Rama's own mother, grieved deeply but did not stop him from honoring the vow?
questions.explanation: Kausalya, Rama's mother, was overcome with sorrow yet ultimately blessed him as he departed to keep dharma.

questions.q: Which river did Rama cross with Guha's help as he journeyed away from Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Guha the Nishada chief arranged a boat to ferry Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana across the river Ganga.

questions.q: Which sage at Chitrakuta or along the way advised Rama on where to dwell during the early exile?
questions.explanation: The sage Valmiki, in whose hermitage tradition places part of the journey, guided Rama, and later composed the epic itself.

questions.q: What was the name of the royal capital river beside which Ayodhya stood, where the citizens mourned Rama's departure?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya stood on the banks of the river Sarayu, where grieving citizens followed Rama as he left for exile.

questions.q: Beside which river did the exiles first camp the night they left Ayodhya, before the citizens followed them?
questions.explanation: On the first night of exile, Rama's party camped on the banks of the Tamasa river, slipping away before the sleeping citizens woke.

questions.q: What did Rama tell Bharata about returning, when Bharata pleaded with him at Chitrakuta?
options: He would return after one year
He must complete the full fourteen years to honor the vow
He would never return to Ayodhya
He would return only if Kaikeyi apologized
questions.explanation: Rama insisted he must fulfill the complete term of fourteen years' exile to uphold his father's word and dharma before returning.
